reasoning: Andriy Govorov has held the 50m butterfly long course world record (22.27) since July 1, 2018. Records are very rarely retroactively stripped without proof of a violation occurring at the exact time the record was set. Furthermore, world records in 50m butterfly are seldom broken outside of peak form periods (typically summer for major international championships). Given the extremely short forecast horizon of 1 to 4 weeks (February 8 to March 3, 2026), the base rate probability of the record falling is very small. I am therefore predicting near the maximum allowable limit, with a slight decrease in probability for the longer horizon to represent the marginally increased risk of a record-breaking swim or an unexpected FINA administrative action.
